reviews.review_text:
A friend and I got this many years ago to play as a non-standard kind of RTS. In some ways the game was great fun and in others very frustrating. As the game picked up years and new DLC were released, issues with connectivity were not fixed, balance issues around new clans were constant, and the core mechanic around the game stayed the same, i.e. you need to econ or you will fail.

You could call this game an RTS, but that would be mistaking how slow this game can be and how clans can go without focusing on troops at all. Games can take hours and that only makes the connectivity issues worse when you are trying to play any multiplayer.

I don't dislike the game, but it seems to be lacking the love it needs to not be chore to play at times. A frustrating game stops being enjoyable, which is why my friend and I have stopped playing and why I would not recommend this game to others.

A brilliant Viking survival RTS where the winter is far deadlier than your enemies.

At first glance, Northgard might look like a standard, streamlined real-time strategy game, but beneath the snow lies a brilliantly balanced experience that perfectly nails the "easy to learn, hard to master" philosophy. The core mechanics of managing your settlement, assigning villagers, and expanding your territory are beautifully simple and intuitive. You won't need to memorize a massive tech tree just to survive your first year. However, the true depth of the game reveals itself in the clan system. Depending on the faction you choose, your entire playstyle, economy, and win conditions shift dramatically, offering layers of engaging complexity that keep every single match feeling fresh.

Unlike many modern RTS games that treat their single-player campaigns as glorified, hastily thrown-together tutorials, the story mode here is genuinely fantastic. It weaves a compelling Norse saga of revenge, survival, and conquest that keeps you invested from start to finish. The campaign does an excellent job of organically introducing the different clans and their unique mechanics while throwing distinct, challenging scenarios your way. It is a deeply satisfying narrative journey that stands incredibly strong on its own, even if you never intend to touch the multiplayer modes.

What makes Northgard such a phenomenal overall RTS is how it balances traditional combat with harsh environmental survival. You aren't just fighting rival factions, draugr, or mythical beasts; you are constantly battling the map itself. A harsh blizzard, an earthquake, or a sudden rat infestation can be just as devastating as an enemy raiding party. It creates a highly polished strategy experience that doesn't rely on frantic, 300-actions-per-minute clicking to win, but rather rewards smart planning, adaptability, and careful resource management.

10/10 — My bravest warriors didn't die in glorious battle; they starved to death because I forgot to assign a farmer before December.

I have over 100 hours, because I have been with this game for a long time. I always treated it as early access, that one day they will get it figured out.  It's clear they completely stopped development years ago, and instead just release DLC.  
Not even a Save and Quit option to continue a play through later. It's insanely poorly featured and buggy.  Sad misguided studio.

Ive played this for years on xbox and I never thought it would turn out the way it did, they use to have controller support on steam but that went away. No it doesn't feel like the same game. this new variation of whatever this is is not what I played 5 years ago. I used to have fun but not anymore. Unfortunately this game has deteriorated.

I've played this game for 2,900 hours. I've been playing for several years and used to love it. But the update that changed the ranked game system is a real nightmare. You used to choose the lobby you wanted to play in, you chose your team, and players usually managed to balance the ranked clans themselves. The new system is terrible. If you didn't like the clans or players, you'd simply leave the lobby. Now, after choosing your clan, you can't leave the lobby, which causes people to quit the game during the server loading screen and never join the desperate game. Why is there a game start? If not all players are present, such a game should automatically pause immediately, instead of me having to wait after starting and waste several minutes waiting for a window to exit without losing points. Another tragedy is the players' approach to ranked play. Back in the day, you'd play until the last villager was dead. Today, even the smallest thing is enough for a player to quit ranked play and leave. It's very sad. I believe the penalty system for quitting should be very strict, so that players want to play until the end, and not just leave for the slightest reason, because it's frustrating. I remember a few years ago, winning a game 2 vs. 4 or 1 vs. 2. Now, it's very difficult to play well until the last drop of blood because if something goes wrong, people immediately leave the game. The new ranking system also doesn't allow for clan balancing; you choose and can't change. It's a disaster when playing with randoms. Everyone wants to play their favorite clan, but if the opposing team suddenly plays an offensive clan, you can't change your clan because there's a lock. In the past, you could enter the clan you were playing without in the ranked lobby name, and the host had greater player selection options. Today, that's gone. I remember that games without the raven and wolf were popular back in the day, because some people preferred to play ranked games more calmly without the pressure of the wolf. The Hounds clan is a disaster. The creators must have gotten the game's genesis wrong. How could they introduce such a completely different and divergent clan into this game? The clan's balance is terrible. I feel like this clan is completely out of balance; you're either too weak or too strong. Simply killing warriors who regenerate health during combat is also irritating. The removal of the 4v4 mode from ranked play is also sad. I used to love this game, but unfortunately not anymore, the new ranked mode ruined it, because the host had no way to influence the game, players often leave the battlefield after the first battle, there used to be many such battles in one game...
hmm tried to play this again after I lost interest due to lack of innovation/ bug fixes unfortunately this turned into a cash grab game

This is a great game, and a unique take on the genre. People these days are very hard to please. If a game strikes out and does something unique, then people complain about the learning curve. They complain that the game is not like other games or the mechanics are too difficult or tedious to master. Then, if the game is a carbon copy of something else, they rightly complain about it being unoriginal, and non-innovative. Can't please everyone.

This is an RTS, albeit different to what your probably used to. In terms of being "incredibly slow paced", that really depends upon the level of challenge your facing in multiplayer or your difficulty level in singleplayer.

There's a decent amount of challenge here in singleplayer if you play on extreme difficulty as I did. It's not starcraft 2 and you shouldn't expect it to be. But people complaining about the game being 'incredibly slow' and yet also complaining about the difficulty of the game, are really just telling everyone without telling everyone how bad they are at the game. This is in my opinion an incredibly easy and casual game if you're playing on anything except extreme difficulty in singleplayer. There is a decent RTS/micromanagement challenge if you're on extreme difficulty and optimizing managing economy with responding to invasions from wolves, or attacking enemies and microing to optimize unit health for fights. But yes, it is primarily an economy and planning orientated game, combat is rather limited in depth.

The first campaign at least the first few missions were good. I wasn't a big fan of the latter missions or the second campaign, although the second campaign is interesting in introducing new mechanics. The best challenge you will get out the singleplayer experience is extreme difficulty in solo conquest mode. I can't report on the multiplayer experience, some have reported that servers are down and the game has been abandoned on that front. If multi-player is important to you, you might second-guess buying to this. But as a singleplayer experience for an more planning/eco based RTS, I can recommend this.
I like it. It's simple and pretty easy to get in to. The campaign is decent, some parts are challenging at first but not impossible.

I loved this game from the begininng, however, I haven't been so keen on some of the newer clans. I think it's because I prefer the older mechanics and I don't want to have to learn anything new :P
But I still keep coming back; over 500 hours now and its a favourite whne I don't know what I'm in the mood for.

I enjoy the game, its pretty chill and can be sweaty, im still new though so i know there's more to learn. I use the stag clan mostly because I have no clue what else to use that's good. The military mechanics are quite good and its a good strategy game.
The best and most fun strategy + battles game! I own all of the DLCs, clans and so much love this game, I play it it over and over. Over 1400 hours of pure enjoynment! <3

so this game is worthy. but fair warning if you buy the "definitive" edition youd think you got the whole game, but this is not true. first thing i noticed is that youre short a bunch of clans (as in theres more clans you dont have than do) so thats a kick in the teeth. havent got into the story yet as ive just been doing skirmish maps. which thats another thing, there needs to be more control and options to map manipulation. because in current state just feels like random map and no control over it other than size. music seems to lack a bit as well as it its more like elevator music than music to the viking theme and doesnt really change regardless of if youre in combat or just playing. I do really like that the gameplay is a mashup game of civilization and age of empires which gives the game a cool unique gameplay to it that i praise the devs for. all in all if you get the game on sale for the $8 USD youll get your moneys worth forsure but probably wont come back to it if you dont pay for other clans or have friends to play with.

I still find this game really fun and engaging in PVP and Conquest. Love some of the new clans and their flexibility/new mechanics.
The only issue I can see with it is power creep with DLC clans and older beloved clans falling behind or being nerfed into the ground because of the newer clans (Raven, Bear, Ox, Stag)
